summary: Recursive downward search for the largest proper divisor on i64 (largest_divisor_at n d tests d, d-1, ... terminating at 1); strong induction via Nat.strongRecOn on the descending measure d.toInt.toNat proves one bundled postcondition (∃ r, 1 ≤ r < n ∧ r ∣ n ∧ ∀ k, r < k ≤ d → ¬ k ∣ n), from which the three Rust contract clauses (divides / strictly-less-than / maximality) are projected — signed mod bridged Int64.toInt_mod → Int.tmod_eq_emod_of_nonneg → Int.dvd_of_emod_eq_zero (and converse via Int.emod_eq_zero_of_dvd), partial %? discharged by casing on n=Int64.minValue && d=-1 and d=0, partial -? discharged via BitVec.ssubOverflow + Int64.subOverflow_iff, and the maximality clause is propagated through recursion by case-splitting k = d.toInt (contradicts n%d ≠ 0) vs k ≤ d.toInt - 1 (IH).
